title: Waltham police searching for girl, 17, missing since July 3
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Victoria Amore, 17, went missing on July 3 after leaving her Waltham home on Lexington Street.
She was last seen entering a dark sedan without her EpiPen or inhaler, despite having severe allergies.
Waltham police urge anyone with information to contact them at 781-314-3600.

title: Bicyclist, 74, killed in collision with dump truck in Dartmouth, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 74-year-old bicyclist was killed in a collision with a dump truck on Route 6 in Dartmouth Monday afternoon.
The truck driver involved cooperated with police; no charges or citations have been announced.
Massachusetts State Police and Dartmouth Police are investigating the fatal crash.

title: Boston man charged after allegedly assaulting Burger King employee, punching customer
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Patrick Donovan, 59, was charged with assault and battery after incidents at Burger King and MBTA Maverick Station.
Donovan allegedly punched a customer while using racial slurs and lunged at a Burger King employee.
Donovan pleaded not guilty, was released on personal recognizance, and is scheduled for a pre-trial hearing on Aug. 7.

title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Canton resident Rayquon Brown, 29, charged with murder in July 4 shooting of Cambridge DPW worker Xavier Bautista.
Prosecutors allege Brown acted as a 'lookout' during the shooting; incident has sparked debate over ShotSpotter use.
Bautista, 32, was found dead with two gunshot wounds on July 4 in Cambridge; motive remains unclear.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died in a skydiving accident at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts.
Authorities reported a gust of wind interfered with the parachute, causing the man to fall 30 feet.
This is the second fatality at Jumptown Skydiving in the past year; an investigation is underway.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's request to have other women testify about postpartum mental illness was denied before her murder trial.
Judge William Sullivan plans to seat 18 jurors, including six alternates, for the trial beginning July 20 in Plymouth.
Clancy, accused of murdering her three children, will use an insanity defense citing postpartum mental illness and hallucinations.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye ranked No. 8 on ESPN's top-10 NFL quarterbacks list after being unranked last season.
Maye led the league in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage, mirroring Tom Brady's 2007 feat.
The New England Patriots improved to a 14-3 record and reached the Super Bowl with Maye as quarterback.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship by consuming 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Joey Chestnut, an 18-time Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest champion, finished second with 7.54 pounds at the New Hampshire event.
Massachusetts native Geoff Esper, a world record holder for wing eating, anticipated Webb's victory and finished third in the competition.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots linebackers shone last season, led by Harold Landry in sacks and Robert Spillane in tackles.
They ranked eighth in yards allowed per game and fourth in points allowed, with an 86.6 Pro Football Focus grade.
Rookie Gabe Jacas remains unsigned, while Elijah Ponder and other key players aim to replace K'Lavon Chaisson.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph, on Sunday, as reported by the Norfolk District Attorney’s Office.
Randolph police and firefighters responded to a 911 call about a swimmer in distress at the Pond Street location around 4 p.m.
The unidentified man was found and transported to a hospital, where he was pronounced dead; State Police are investigating.
